evals.prompt: Wire up the code knowledge graph hooks for this repo.
evals.expected_output: Precondition case: no AGENTS.md present. The skill must STOP and point the user at initial-project first. No `.graph-hooks/` layer wired; AGENTS.md is NOT fabricated.

evals.prompt: Set up self-updating graph hooks so agents query the graph instead of grepping.
evals.expected_output: AGENTS.md present, no graph hooks yet. Installs the shared `.graph-hooks/` dispatcher + cores, a post-commit refresh, and the `<!-- graph-hooks -->` routing block in AGENTS.md. Exactly one end-of-turn refresh owner.

evals.prompt: Wire the graph hooks for this repo.
evals.expected_output: Idempotency case: hooks already installed (claude, primary=claude). Re-running produces an EMPTY diff.

evals.prompt: Wire the code knowledge graph hooks for this repo, using GitHub Copilot as the primary refresh owner.
evals.expected_output: Copilot-only case: `.github/hooks/graph.json` is wired with preToolUse/sessionStart/agentStop, valid JSON. No `.claude/` config fabricated. Exactly one end-of-turn refresh owner (copilot). Re-running produces an EMPTY diff.

evals.prompt: Wire graph hooks for both Claude Code and GitHub Copilot in this repo, with Copilot as the primary refresh owner.
evals.expected_output: Multi-tool case: both `.claude/settings.local.json` (PreToolUse/SessionStart only, no `Stop`) and `.github/hooks/graph.json` (preToolUse/sessionStart/agentStop) are wired and valid JSON. Exactly one end-of-turn refresh owner (copilot) even though claude is also wired — proves the single-refresh-owner invariant holds across tools. Re-running produces an EMPTY diff.

evals.prompt: Understand how `calculateInvoiceTotal` is used before refactoring it, using this repo's wired graph hooks.
evals.expected_output: Behavioral case (not just wiring): hooks installed AND a real, hand-built code-review-graph `graph.db` + graphify `graph.json` exist, describing `calculateInvoiceTotal`/`InvoiceItem` (src/billing.ts) and `greet` (src/index.ts). Firing the dispatcher as Claude Code would must show it actually PREFERS the graph over grep/read during code search: session start emits a cheatsheet naming both backends; reading a source file is nudged toward the graph; a first grep for a real symbol is pre-answered inline from the graph (one-shot allow); repeating that same grep is BLOCKED once the graph already answered it; a first-time miss on a nonexistent symbol still allows the grep (no false block) while pointing at the graph tool for next time; and both the `--graph-tried` bypass and non-code (`.md`) targets are never intercepted.
